questions.question: Explain the components of the viewing coordinate system and their purposes.
keyPoints: View Reference Point (VRP) - origin of viewing system
View Up Vector (VUP) - defines upward direction
Projection Reference Point (PRP) - for projection rays

questions.question: Differentiate between perspective projection and parallel projection with examples of their applications.
keyPoints: Perspective: vanishing points, depth perception, used in animation films
Parallel: parallel lines preserved, used in engineering drawings
Key difference: convergence of parallel lines

questions.question: Describe the window-to-viewport transformation process.
keyPoints: Window defines portion in world coordinates

questions.question: What is a viewing frustum? List its components and explain their significance.
keyPoints: 6 clipping planes: near, far, left, right, top, bottom
Bound by camera position and view plane

questions.question: Explain the complete viewing pipeline in computer graphics.
What is the purpose of normalization transformation in the viewing pipeline?
keyPoints: Transforms arbitrary view volume to canonical form

questions.question: Define orthographic projection and state its advantages in technical drawing.
keyPoints: Projection rays perpendicular to view plane

questions.question: Derive and explain the matrix representation for a general viewing transformation in 3D computer graphics. Include explanation of how world coordinates are transformed to viewing coordinates.
keyPoints: Step 2: Align VPN with -Z axis using rotation
Step 3: Align VUP with Y axis
Viewing matrix converts world coords to camera coords

questions.question: Explain the concept of perspective projection with its mathematical formulation. How does it create the perception of depth? Derive the perspective transformation matrix.
keyPoints: Mathematical formulation: x' = x/z, y' = y/z
How parallel lines appear to converge

questions.question: Discuss the various types of clipping planes used in the viewing system and their role in the rendering pipeline. Explain how clipping is performed in relation to the viewing frustum.
keyPoints: Side clipping planes - left, right, top, bottom

questions.question: Compare one-point, two-point, and three-point perspective projections. Explain their applications and how they are achieved mathematically.
keyPoints: One-point: one vanishing point, viewing perpendicular to object face
Two-point: two vanishing points, corner view
Three-point: three vanishing points, bird's eye or worm's eye view
